type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I'm implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update(). I only need src/datasets/features/features.py. Please tell me which file(s) your feature touches so we can confirm disjoint sets.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implementing keep_features_dicts_synced decorator for Features class (sync _column_requires_decoding dict). Only touching features.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py (unavoidable overlap). My change is scoped to the Features.update() method only. Yours is a decorator syncing _column_requires_decoding. These should be non-overlapping regions of the file. I'll proceed now; we'll merge branches via git before final patch. Ping me if you touch update() so we can avoid clobbering.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py (Features class), so a disjoint split isn't literally possible. Proposal: I'll add a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on the Features class that wraps __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding, but I will NOT change the deep-merge semantics inside update()'s loop body - I'll keep it as simple self[key]=value assignment. You independently implement deep-merge logic in update() (e.g. merging nested dict values). We'll each work on our own branch, and during the final git merge step reconcile any textual conflict on the update() method by combining: my decorator wrapping + your deep-merge body. Are you ok with this, or would you rather one of us implement both changes together in one function to avoid merge pain?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement deep-merge logic inside update()'s loop body only, leaving decorator/wrapping concerns to you. Will keep my diff scoped narrowly to that method so merge is easy. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Confirmed. I'll implement keep_features_dicts_synced decorator wrapping __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear, keeping update()'s internal loop body (the self[key]=value assignment logic) untouched so your deep-merge changes there don't conflict. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator, applied to __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear. update()'s body is untouched (still the self[key]=value loop) so your deep-merge logic can go inside that loop without conflicting with the decorator itself - just expect a git merge conflict on the update() method text since we both touch it; happy to help resolve. Tests pass locally.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented deep-merge in Features.update() via new _update_value/_deep_merge_dicts helper methods, scoped to update()'s body (added right after the existing update method, before __reduce__). Didn't touch __setitem__/__delitem__ or add any decorator. Ready to merge whenever you are.
